summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on two cases: BZA 2025-004 (conditional use for an accessory dwelling unit) and BZA 2025-005 (variance for exceeding maximum square footage on an accessory dwelling). Both cases were filed by Keith Benhase of 5507 Aviator Avenue for a proposed apartment within his existing pole barn for his parents. Both the conditional use and the variance were approved unanimously.
decisions: Approved video meeting minutes of March 17 (2 yes, 1 abstain)
Approved conditional use (BZA 2025-004) for accessory dwelling unit at 5507 Aviator Avenue with condition that it be used for immediate family use only
Approved variance (BZA 2025-005) to exceed 800 sq ft maximum by 204 sq ft for the accessory dwelling unit at 5507 Aviator Avenue
Motion to adjourn approved; BZA closed at 6:57 PM
action_items: John Lang and zoning staff to follow up with Keith Benhase on next steps for zoning permits
Board seeking an alternate BZA member; members asked to refer candidates
Zoning Workshop scheduled for Thursday at 6 PM in the same room
key_amounts: Proposed accessory dwelling unit: 1,004 square feet
Maximum allowed accessory dwelling size: 800 square feet
Variance requested: 204 square feet over maximum
224 square foot addition to existing barn
notable_topics: Conditional use approval for accessory dwelling unit (BZA 2025-004)
Variance for exceeding maximum accessory dwelling square footage (BZA 2025-005)
Condition that accessory dwelling be used for immediate family only
Applicant's intent to convert dwelling back to barn after parents no longer need it
notes: Mr. Shaw was called during roll call but did not respond (absent). The transcript has significant portions of garbled/corrupted text likely due to automated transcription errors. The BZA meeting opened at 6:30 PM and closed at 6:57 PM. The applicant is a former police department employee recognized by board members.
title: BZA 5-19-25
